retrieved_context.title: Trust boundary analysis for web UI
retrieved_context.text: A device web interface represents a trust boundary between user and system. Weak authentication or unencrypted communication reduces this boundary and increases exposure risk.

retrieved_context.title: Transport security implications
retrieved_context.text: If the web interface uses unencrypted HTTP, credentials and session tokens may be exposed to local attackers. This directly weakens trust boundaries between user and device.

retrieved_context.title: Trust boundary in local network
retrieved_context.text: Local network exposure should not be assumed safe. Devices must enforce proper authentication even within internal networks.

retrieved_context.title: Configuration exposure reasoning
retrieved_context.text: Administrative interfaces should be restricted and protected. If configuration endpoints are accessible without strong controls, this increases risk of unauthorized modification.

tutor_output.hint: Consider exploring whether the login page enforces strong authentication and if the communication channel is encrypted or not.
tutor_output.rationale: The web interface acts as a trust boundary. Weak authentication or unencrypted HTTP can expose credentials and session data to attackers, weakening this boundary and increasing risk.
tutor_output.check_question: What methods can you use to determine if the login mechanism is strong and if the communication is encrypted?

assistant_output.suggestion: Examine whether the login process relies on weak or default credentials and verify if authentication controls are enforced properly.
assistant_output.explanation: Weak authentication reduces the effectiveness of the trust boundary between the user and the device's administrative interface, increasing risks of unauthorized access. Assessing credential strength and authentication enforcement is critical to identifying insecure configuration.
assistant_output.safety_note: Perform only non-intrusive verification steps within the authorised lab environment and avoid any actions that could disrupt device functionality or violate lab policies.
